reason: The statement applies because the protagonist Gideon can be seen as a reluctant hero who starts off as a humble man threshing wheat to hide it from the Midianites, but is chosen by God to lead the Israelites in battle against their oppressors. Gideon questions God's plan multiple times and doubts his own abilities, which fits the description of a tragicomic figure. Ultimately, Gideon's actions lead to the defeat of the Midianites and the restoration of Israelite power, showcasing elements of a heroic narrative.
